title: H2 L2 Pullback Continuation Framework
focus_questions: Is this still a valid trend pullback, or has the market already degraded into range behavior?
Is the second entry forming at a meaningful continuation location, or is the count being forced inside noise?

summary.thesis: H2 and L2 continuation entries work when the trend is still intact, the pullback is still corrective, and the second attempt comes from a meaningful location.
summary.operator_translation: Do not count bars blindly. Count only when the trend still exists and the second attempt is testing a real continuation location.
summary.ai_usage_note: Use this card when pullback counting helps separate valid continuation from broad-channel degradation or range recycling.
summary.ai_brief: Environment: established trend or clear trend leg in range. Required evidence: real prior trend, corrective pullback, valid second attempt, and intact trend origin. Do not trade: by counting endlessly in chop, from the middle of a range, or after trend origin already broke. Real trigger: trend-aligned second entry from a meaningful location. Failure point: the second attempt also fails and the pullback degrades into range or reversal.

when_relevant: A clear trend or trend leg existed before the pullback began.
The pullback is still acting corrective rather than accepted reversal.
The operator is deciding whether a first or second continuation attempt is still valid.
required_evidence: there is a clear prior trend, breakout leg, or bull-bear leg in a range
the pullback remains corrective instead of becoming broad and fully overlapping
the counted H2 or L2 is based on a real failed first attempt rather than random bar labeling
trend origin or key structural support-resistance has not been objectively broken
confirmation_signals: the second attempt forms from a meaningful location such as EMA, breakout point, channel edge, or prior swing reference
the signal bar is aligned with the trend direction and continuation begins to resume
trend travel restarts without immediate collapse back into the pullback
invalidation_signals: trend origin is broken and the pullback has already become a deeper reversal candidate
the count keeps extending into H3 or L3 wedge behavior without resumption
the market broadens into range behavior and the counted entry loses location advantage
no_trade_conditions: Do not count endlessly inside chop and call it H2 or L2.
Do not treat a weak H1 or L1 as if it were equal to a strong H2 or L2.
Do not use this framework after trend origin is already broken.
Do not open from the middle of a range just because a count exists.

entry_archetypes.name: second entry breakout
entry_archetypes.description: Enter as the trend-aligned second attempt triggers from a corrective pullback.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the signal bar is weak or the pullback already looks broad and range-like.

entry_archetypes.name: continuation retest after second attempt
entry_archetypes.description: Enter on the first retest after H2 or L2 has already triggered and continuation proves it can hold.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the retest immediately breaks trend origin or collapses back into deep overlap.

management_notes: Second entries are often better than first entries because the first countertrend attempt already failed.
If the second attempt also fails, reassess whether the market is transitioning into range or reversal.
The best versions usually test a meaningful continuation reference instead of forming in open-space noise.
review_questions: Was the market still in valid continuation context or already in range?
Was the H2 or L2 count based on real attempts, or was it forced onto noise?
Did the entry come from a meaningful continuation location?
Did the operator confuse second entry logic with certainty?
